pairConnect.lanDevicesDesc: Android wireless debugging devices discovered via mDNS
pairConnect.firstTimeHint: If this is the first time connecting this device, please enable pairing code in Android wireless debugging and complete pairing first.
pairConnect.autoConnectNone: Auto-connect attempted, no online devices found
pairConnect.connectPortPlaceholder: Port shown on wireless debugging page
pairConnect.howToGetPairCode: How to get the pairing code
pairConnect.guideStep1: On your Android device, open Settings → Developer options → Wireless debugging
pairConnect.guideStep3: Tap Pair device with pairing code
pairConnect.guideStep4: The screen will display a 6-digit pairing code and pairing port number — enter them in the pairing section above
pairConnect.howToGetConnectAddr: How to get the connection IP and port
pairConnect.guideConnectStep1: On your Android device, open Settings → Developer options → Wireless debugging
pairConnect.guideConnectStep2: The IP address and port displayed on the page is the connection address (note: this port differs from the pairing port)
pairConnect.guideConnectStep3: Enter the IP and port in the connection section above
pairConnect.guideTip: Tip: Pairing is only needed once — subsequent connections don't require re-pairing. LAN scanning will automatically discover devices and fill in the addresses. If a device can't be found, make sure wireless debugging is enabled in Android developer options, and try switching Wi-Fi and rescanning.
pairConnect.noDeviceHintTitle: If no devices are found, use manual connection
pairConnect.noDeviceHintDesc: Check the IP address and port on Android's wireless debugging page, and enter them in the "Connect Device" section below. This port changes — don't use old ports or the pairing port from the pairing code dialog.
pairConnect.repairNoDevice: {{message}}, but no devices were found. Check the current IP and port shown on Android's wireless debugging page, then try manual connection.
pairConnect.multiNetworkHint: This computer has multiple LAN addresses: {{ips}}. Make sure the Android device is on the same subnet. If the device shows 192.168.110.x, use the current port from that page for manual connection.

screenMirror.title: Remote Control
screenMirror.scrcpyRequired: Remote control requires scrcpy. On macOS, it will auto-detect and install Homebrew, then run brew install scrcpy; on Windows, it will automatically download scrcpy and install it to the local app directory.
screenMirror.warmTipDesc: macOS mirroring requires Homebrew and scrcpy; Windows mirroring requires scrcpy. The one-click install below will try to set everything up automatically. If installation fails, please visit the official pages and follow the latest instructions, then return to this page.
screenMirror.openInteractive: Open interactive screen mirror via scrcpy
screenMirror.audioCaptureDesc: Off by default to avoid startup failures on Android devices that do not support scrcpy AudioRecord capture. Enable it to try mirroring device audio.
screenMirror.selectDevice: Please select an online device first
screenMirror.note1: The mirror window is provided by scrcpy — you can interact with the device using mouse and keyboard directly
screenMirror.note2: Back and Home can be triggered via the buttons above, or by right-clicking or middle-clicking in the mirror window
screenMirror.note3: For screenshots and recordings, use the dedicated Screenshot and Record tabs above

imageCast.title: Image Cast
imageCast.description: Push a local reference image to the device and open it with the system image viewer.
imageCast.noDevice: Please select an online device first
imageCast.dropHint: Drop a PNG, JPG, JPEG, or WebP image
imageCast.unsupportedFile: Please choose a PNG, JPG, JPEG, or WebP image
imageCast.previewFailed: Image preview failed, but you can still try pushing it

screenshot.title: Device Screenshot
screenshot.shortcutHint: Press the global shortcut to take a screenshot without opening the preview. ADB Manager must be running.
screenshot.noSaveDir: Please configure the screenshot save directory in Settings first
screenshot.openFolderFailed: Failed to open folder — please navigate to the save directory manually
screenshot.noDevice: No device selected, using default device

screenRecord.title: Device Recording
screenRecord.noSaveDir: Please configure the recording save directory in Settings first
screenRecord.nearingLimit: Recording is approaching the 3-minute limit — the device will stop automatically
screenRecord.openFolderFailed: Failed to open folder — please navigate to the save directory manually
screenRecord.note1: Device recording is limited to 3 minutes (Android system limit)
screenRecord.note3: After clicking "Stop Recording", the file will be saved automatically

clipboard.title: Clipboard
clipboard.placeholder: Enter or paste text to send to the device's current input field
clipboard.noDevice: No device selected, using default device

workbench.title: ADB Workbench
workbench.subtitle: Use preset abilities, reusable templates, or custom ADB subcommands without opening Terminal.
workbench.templatesDesc: Saved reusable commands appear here for quick selection and execution.
workbench.customDesc: Enter the part after adb. The selected device is added automatically.
workbench.noDevice: No device selected. ADB will use its default device selection.
workbench.highRiskConfirm: I understand this command may delete data, uninstall apps, reboot the device, or make destructive changes.

deviceProps.title: Read all device properties
deviceProps.desc: Run getprop to inspect build, product, and system properties.

deviceModel.title: Read device model
deviceModel.desc: Get ro.product.model from the selected device.

appList.title: List installed packages
appList.desc: Show installed package names on the device.

installApk.title: Install APK
installApk.desc: Run adb install with replace, grant, downgrade, or test APK options.

installExisting.title: Install existing package
installExisting.desc: Run cmd package install-existing for a package already present on the device.

listPermissions.title: List dangerous permissions
listPermissions.desc: Run pm list permissions -g -d to inspect grouped dangerous permissions.

clearData.title: Clear app data
clearData.desc: Run pm clear. This signs users out and deletes local app data.

forceStop.title: Force stop app
forceStop.desc: Stop a running app process by package name.

startActivity.title: Start activity
startActivity.desc: Launch an app activity with am start -n.

grant.title: Grant permission
grant.desc: Grant a runtime permission to an app package.

setprop.title: Set system property
setprop.desc: Run shell setprop for device/system feature flags.

dumpsysActivity.title: Inspect top activity
dumpsysActivity.desc: Run dumpsys activity top for foreground app diagnostics.

keyevent.title: Send key event
keyevent.desc: Send Android keyevent values such as Home, Back, or Power.

inputText.title: Input text
inputText.desc: Type text into the currently focused field on the device.

logcatSearch.title: Dump logcat
logcatSearch.desc: Read current logcat output, optionally scoped by tag/query.

deviceSerial.title: Read device serial
deviceSerial.desc: Read ro.serialno to identify the physical device.

displaySize.title: Read display size
displaySize.desc: Run wm size to inspect physical and override sizes.

displayDensity.title: Read display density
displayDensity.desc: Run wm density to inspect DPI settings.

currentFocus.title: Inspect window focus
currentFocus.desc: Run dumpsys window to diagnose focused window and Activity state.

screenshot.title: Take device screenshot
screenshot.desc: Run screencap -p and save the screenshot to a device path.

screenrecord.title: Record device screen
screenrecord.desc: Run screenrecord --time-limit and save the recording to a device path.

listDirectory.title: List device directory
listDirectory.desc: Run ls -la to inspect folders such as /sdcard and Download on the device.

pullFile.title: Pull file
pullFile.desc: Run adb pull to copy a file from the device to this computer.

pushFile.title: Push file
pushFile.desc: Run adb push to copy a local file to a device path.

networkIp.title: Inspect network addresses
networkIp.desc: Run ip addr to list network interface addresses.

networkRoute.title: Inspect routes
networkRoute.desc: Run ip route to inspect default routes and network egress.

wifiStatus.title: Inspect Wi-Fi status
wifiStatus.desc: Run dumpsys wifi for Wi-Fi connection diagnostics.

appPath.title: Read APK path
appPath.desc: Run pm path for a package.

packageDump.title: Inspect package details
packageDump.desc: Run dumpsys package for a package.

monkeyLaunch.title: Launch app by package
monkeyLaunch.desc: Use monkey to launch the package's Launcher Activity.

disableApp.title: Disable app
disableApp.desc: Run pm disable-user for the package.

enableApp.title: Enable app
enableApp.desc: Run pm enable for the package.

uninstall.title: Uninstall app
uninstall.desc: Run adb uninstall to remove the app.

revoke.title: Revoke permission
revoke.desc: Revoke a runtime permission from an app package.

enableComponent.title: Enable component
enableComponent.desc: Enable an Activity, Receiver, or Service component.

disableComponent.title: Disable component
disableComponent.desc: Disable an Activity, Receiver, or Service component.

meminfo.title: Inspect app memory
meminfo.desc: Run dumpsys meminfo for a package.

cpuTop.title: Inspect CPU usage
cpuTop.desc: Run top -n 1 to snapshot process CPU usage.

diskUsage.title: Inspect disk usage
diskUsage.desc: Run df -h to inspect device partition usage.

storage.title: Inspect storage stats
storage.desc: Run dumpsys diskstats to inspect Android storage statistics.

dumpsysInput.title: Inspect input system
dumpsysInput.desc: Run dumpsys input to diagnose touch and input state.

swipe.title: Swipe coordinates
swipe.desc: Run input swipe x1 y1 x2 y2 duration.

logcatClear.title: Clear logcat buffer
logcatClear.desc: Run logcat -c to clear the current log buffer.

logcat.title: ADB Logcat
logcat.autoRefresh: Auto-refreshing every 1 minute — you can also refresh manually
logcat.readLatest: Reads the latest 1000 lines each time
logcat.noDevice: No device selected, using default device

apkInstall.title: Install APK
apkInstall.dropHint: Drag APKs or folders containing APKs here, or click Select Files
apkInstall.pasteHint: You can also copy APKs or folders in Finder, then return here and press Cmd/Ctrl+V or click Paste.
apkInstall.searchHint: Typing searches automatically; click a suggested package to select it
apkInstall.forceInstallDesc: Force install will uninstall the existing package first, then install the current APK
apkInstall.parseFailed: Could not auto-detect package name — please enter it manually for force install
apkInstall.parseFailedNonForce: Could not auto-detect package name; normal install can still continue
apkInstall.forceNeedPkg: Force install requires a package name
apkInstall.forceNeedPkgMultiple: Force install requires a package name for every APK
apkInstall.forceMultiDesc: Multi-APK force install uninstalls each detected package before installing that APK
apkInstall.noDevice: No device selected, using default device
apkInstall.note1: Default mode is overlay install (preserves data). Check "Force install" to clear data.
apkInstall.note2: Force install uninstalls the old version first (data will be lost), then installs the new version
apkInstall.note3: Installation may require confirming install permissions on the device
apkInstall.note4: Multiple APKs are installed sequentially; failures are recorded and the next APK continues

packageList.selectDevice: Please select an online device first

adbSetup.desc1: ADB (Android Debug Bridge) is a required tool for connecting and managing Android devices.
adbSetup.desc2: Click the button below to install it.
adbSetup.windows: Windows: Download Platform Tools from the Android developer website
